text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音慢速英语健康报道。

text: Picture a huge public gathering, a sea of people like the Hajj to Mecca or an appearance by the Pope.
zh: 想象一下一场大规模的公众聚会,人山人海,比如麦加朝圣或教皇露面的场合。

text: Think of the World Cup, the Olympics, a political event or a rock concert.
zh: 想想世界杯、奥运会、政治活动或摇滚音乐会。

text: When thousands or even millions of people get together,
zh: 当成千上万甚至数百万人聚集在一起时,

text: what do you suppose is the biggest health concern?
zh: 你认为最大的健康隐患是什么?

text: Traditionally, doctors and public health officials
zh: 传统上,医生和公共卫生官员

text: were most concerned about the spread of infectious diseases like influenza.
zh: 最担心的是流感等传染病的传播。

text: Robert Steffen, a researcher in in Switzerland
zh: 瑞士研究员罗伯特·斯特凡

text: says infectious diseases are still a concern.
zh: 表示传染病仍然令人担忧。

text: But he says injuries and hot weather
zh: 但他说,受伤和高温天气

text: are bigger threats at so -called mass gatherings.
zh: 在所谓的大型聚会中是更大的威胁。

text: Mr. Stephan is a professor of travel medicine at the University of Zurich.
zh: 斯特凡先生是苏黎世大学旅行医学教授。

text: He is the lead author of one of several new papers
zh: 他是发表在《柳叶刀·传染病》期刊上

text: about health problems at mass gatherings in the journal Lancet, Infectious Diseases.
zh: 多篇关于大型聚会健康问题新论文中一篇的第一作者。

text: Professor Stephan says children and older people
zh: 斯特凡教授说,儿童和老年人

text: have the highest risk of injury or other health problems at these events.
zh: 在这些活动中受伤或出现其他健康问题的风险最高。

text: He says children are more at risk of getting crushed
zh: 他说,儿童更容易在踩踏事件中

text: in stampedes, while older people are at higher risk from extreme heat.
zh: 被挤压受伤,而老年人则更容易受到极端高温的影响。

text: Stampedes and cases of crushing at mass gatherings
zh: 大型聚会中的踩踏和挤压事件

text: have caused an estimated 7 ,000 deaths over the past 30 years.
zh: 在过去30年里估计造成了7000人死亡。

text: The design of an area can play a part.
zh: 场地的设计也可能起到一定作用。

text: There may be narrow passages or other choke points
zh: 可能存在狭窄通道或其他瓶颈路段,

text: that too many people try to use at once.
zh: 太多人同时试图通过。

text: Professor Stephan says the mood of a crowd can also play a part.
zh: 斯特凡教授说,人群的情绪也可能起作用。

text: For instance, if fireworks are suddenly launched within a football stadium,
zh: 例如,如果在足球场内突然燃放烟花,

text: people can get scared and try to escape.
zh: 人们可能会受到惊吓并试图逃跑。

text: He says He says organizers of large gatherings need to avoid creating conditions
zh: 他说,大型聚会的组织者需要避免制造

text: that might lead to panic, stampedes and heat stroke.
zh: 可能导致恐慌、踩踏和中暑的情况。

text: And he says they must be ready to give medical care.
zh: 他还说,他们必须做好提供医疗救助的准备。

text: So what advice does he have for people attending a large gathering?
zh: 那么,他对参加大型聚会的人有什么建议呢?

text: First, get any needed vaccinations before traveling.
zh: 首先,在出行前接种任何必要的疫苗。

text: Then, stay away from any large mass of people as much as possible.
zh: 然后,尽量远离任何大规模的人群。

text: Also, be careful with alcohol and drugs, which can increase the risk of injuries.
zh: 此外,要谨慎对待酒精和药物,因为它们会增加受伤的风险。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Health Report, available at voaspecialenglish .com,
zh: 以上是美国之音慢速英语健康报道,您可以访问voaspecialenglish.com获取更多内容,

text: with MP3s, PDF files for e -readers, and podcasts.
zh: 包括MP3、供电子阅读器使用的PDF文件以及播客。

text: You could also find our programs on iTunes.
zh: 您也可以在iTunes上找到我们的节目。

text: Have you ever been in a huge crowd and worried about your safety? Or did you feel fine?
zh: 你是否曾置身于巨大的人群中并担心自己的安全?还是感觉一切都好?

text: Post a comment on our website and tell us about your experience.
zh: 请在我们的网站上留言,和我们分享你的经历。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Carolyn Presutti.
zh: 我是卡罗琳·普雷苏蒂,这里是美国之音慢速英语。
